faqs.question: Why are silverfish so common in Grand Concourse apartments?
faqs.answer: Grand Concourse has many pre-war and Art Deco buildings built in the 1920s-1940s with older plumbing systems that can create moisture issues. Silverfish are attracted to humid environments and feed on starches, paper, and fabrics commonly found in these historic apartments. The age and construction of many buildings along the Grand Concourse provide ideal harborage areas for these pests.

faqs.question: What does your silverfish control service include for $350-$550?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive silverfish treatment includes a thorough inspection of your Grand Concourse property, identification of moisture sources and entry points, targeted application of EPA-approved treatments in affected areas, crack and crevice sealing, moisture control recommendations, and a follow-up visit to ensure complete elimination. The price varies based on property size and infestation severity.

We understand that silverfish can damage books, photographs, wallpaper, and clothing, so we prioritize fast response times.

faqs.question: Are your silverfish treatments safe for families and pets in Bronx apartments?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We use EPA-registered products specifically chosen for safety in residential settings, including occupied apartments.
